    "R1.1": "Load-bearing: 'reframe the conceptual story' directly targets the thesis architecture. The artifact has a fractured contribution hierarchy — it declares two separate 'central contributions' (the DiD causal estimate and CrowdAudit) and then appends a third, sweeping normative claim (reconceptualizing the crowdworker as a hybrid agent). Reconstructing which of these is the actual load-bearing thesis, what the true gap statement is, and how the contributions are sequenced is the core deliverable of this delegation.",
    "R1.2": "Central but not load-bearing: any proposed reframe must hold together logically. Checking cross-section coherence in the new conceptual frame is an expected responsibility.",
    "R1.4": "Central: the conceptual story is partly a positioning story. The artifact's novelty claim is currently diffuse — the DiD identification, CrowdAudit, and the hybrid-agent reconceptualization each implicitly compete as the delta vs. prior work. rationale.R1.1: Load-bearing: 'reframe the conceptual story' directly targets the thesis architecture. The artifact has a fractured contribution hierarchy — it declares two separate 'central contributions' (the DiD causal estimate and CrowdAudit) and then appends a third, sweeping normative claim (reconceptualizing the crowdworker as a hybrid agent). Reconstructing which of these is the actual load-bearing thesis, what the true gap statement is, and how the contributions are sequenced is the core deliverable of this delegation.
rationale.R1.2: Central but not load-bearing: any proposed reframe must hold together logically. The artifact's current causal-to-normative leap (from a -0.14 SD estimate to 'wholesale revision of the platform-labor literature') is a logical overextension that a reframe will need to either justify or moderate. Checking cross-section coherence in the new conceptual frame is an expected responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: Central: the conceptual story is partly a positioning story. The artifact's novelty claim is currently diffuse — the DiD identification, CrowdAudit, and the hybrid-agent reconceptualization each implicitly compete as the delta vs. prior work. Clarifying which novelty claim anchors the frame, and how strong a delta it represents, is integral to a coherent reframing.
